Just now

An interactive digital experience designed for custom Yemeni drink creation and express counter pickup.
Duluth Location InteriorSummer 2026 | NMIX 6010 | Web Designer
Project Overview
My final project in this course involved “redesigning” the website for Hayat Coffee Co., a specialty Yemeni coffee shop located in Duluth, Georgia, to create a contemporary and seamless user digital experience while honoring the shop’s rich cultural heritage. By using custom HTML, custom CSS, and some surface-level JavaScript, I explored custom layout grids using a variety of blocks, real-time custom drink pricing calculations, and dark-mode aesthetics to modernize the cafe's online presence without losing the warm, authentic hospitality associated with the physical shop.
The Problem
The original web presence consists of their UberEats for browsing the menu and Instagram for information about the coffee shop, as well as some marketing efforts. Hayat Coffee Co. does not offer their customers an active web presence, especially for first-time guests. Specialty Yemeni menus feature unique regional offerings — such as Qishr (cascara tea), Adeni Spiced Chai, and Saffron Lattes , which can cause confusion or ordering anxiety for unfamiliar customers. Since Hayat Coffee Co. is such a unique cultural staple in Duluth, I wanted to take a shot at redesigning their digital storefront and allow customers to browse their products before coming into the store to familiarize themself with the menu and different variations of each drink.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

The Current “Website”The Solution
I began by analyzing standard cafe templates and exploring pre-made layouts, but something wasn’t right. My biggest challenge at first was exploring with templates, such as the Cafe Coffee Shop theme, and going through extensive trial and error. I was having a hard time fitting in the vision I had for the company’s website into the rigid template and endured styling conflicts that made custom features like a live drink builder difficult to implement cleanly. After experimenting, I realized that the restrictive templates wered detering me from the features I wanted to implement, therefore I decided to step away from pre-built themes and customize using the Twenty-Twenty Five theme I had prior experience with.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

The final redesign features a custom, native layout powered by modern CSS Grid properties, allowing menu cards for online ordering and features containers for a user-friendly experience on desktop and mobile screens. To capture the atmosphere of their physical space and the company’s aesthetic, I incorporated a matte black and warm gold color scheme throughout the website.
To solve the menu familiarity problem and streamline ordering, I engineered an interactive Custom Drink Builder in custom HTML, custom CSS, and basic JavaScript. Users can select base drinks, milk alternatives (Oat, Almond, Coconut), real fruit purees (Mango, Banana, Fig), flavor syrups (Lavender, Rose, Vanilla), and traditional spices (Cardamom, Saffron). The pricing engine calculates the total from scratch on every selection to prevent accumulator bugs, and a Submit Custom Order button copies the formatted order receipt straight to the user’s clipboard for express counter pickup.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Throughout the development process, I also ensured to incorporate supporting utility features, such as a live store status gauge synced to the approximate wait time for the customer’s orders.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Results
I believe my attempt at redesigning the website created a user-friendly experience for first-time and returning customers, as well as telling a little bit more about Hayat Coffee Co. There is definitely room for improvement, such as integrating a database for real-time inventory tracking, that is being updated throughout the day, and actual determined wait times. Therefore, in my future projects, I would work more closely with factors similar to those to create a fully-functional mobile-ordering website. Overall, I would say this project strengthened my skills in basic JavaScript, custom CSS, and custom HTML, while reinforcing the Yemeni coffee shop vibe with the contemporary digital user experience.
